channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Montana Senate race, which could determine majority, seeing 'intense ground game operation': NRSC Chairman
item.description: NRSC Chairman Steve Daines told Fox News Digital in an exclusive interview he is confident Republicans will secure a Senate majority by flipping the Montana Senate seat this cycle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump teases he will 'do everything' while working behind McDonald's counter in crucial swing state
item.description: Former President Trump is making an unusual campaign stop this weekend in battleground Pennsylvania. He will be behind the counter, working the fry cooker at a McDonald's.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Swing-state college students largely support Harris but acknowledge ‘razor-thin’ margin with Trump
item.description: College Democrats staffed a table on Packer Avenue on Lehigh University's campus, and other students passing by generally expressed support for Harris-Walz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judges punishing Jan. 6 rioters say they fear more political violence as Election Day nears
item.description: Judges punishing rioters for storming the U.S. Capitol nearly four years ago fear the nation faces more political violence on the cusp of the next presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris eyes a rural Maine congressional district in a hunt for every possible electoral vote
item.description: One of the largest, coldest and most rural congressional districts in the country could figure prominently in this year’s presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Facing a budget deficit challenge, Johnson aims to safeguard youth and workforce
item.description: Enticing grocery store chains to open in underserved communities is another priority for the Johnson administration.
item.dc:creator: Julian Crews, WGN 9

item.title: Montana GOP candidate who could flip control of Senate nagged by claims he lied about bullet wound
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — A former Park Service ranger said Friday that U.S. Senate hopeful Tim Sheehy of Montana has been lying about a bullet wound that the candidate said came from fighting in Afghanistan — going public with an accusation that has nagged the Republican’s campaign for months.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris mocks pro-life protesters 'at the wrong rally' hours before Catholic charity dinner snub
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris on Thursday told pro-life protesters they were at the "wrong rally" as she campaigned in Wisconsin and skipped the Al Smith Catholic charity dinner.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge unseals heavily redacted trove of evidence in Trump's 2020 election interference case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The judge overseeing Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case made public Friday a heavily redacted trove of documents that provide a small glimpse into the evidence prosecutors will present if the case ever goes to trial.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kentucky Tiktok suit says company considered using KY creators to sway Sen. Mitch McConnell
item.description: The suit says videos by Kentucky small businesses were identified as possibly of interest to the senator, who has expressed concern about the app's effect on kids
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Goodman, MPR News

item.title: Senate Republicans raise questions after funds for disaster loans exhausted
item.description: A group of GOP senators are pressing the Small Business Administration (SBA) for answers after officials said its disaster loan program exhausted its funds earlier this week in the aftermath of a pair of major hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tells child there will be no cows under Harris
item.description: Former President Donald Trump joined Fox and Friends on Friday for an in-person interview where he told a 6-year-old child that the U.S. “won’t have any cows” if Vice President Kamala Harris is elected.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden administration races to shell out billions for clean energy as election nears
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration is shelling out billions of dollars for clean energy and approving major offshore wind projects as officials race to secure major climate initiatives before President Joe Biden's term comes to an end.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT and MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pro-Trump yard sign leads to shooting in Las Vegas neighborhood
item.description: Two men were playing about the former president titled "[Expletive] Donald Trump" when they decided to park their car outside the pro-Trump house.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: What's a 'Jezebel spirit'? Some Christians use the term to paint Kamala Harris with a demonic brush
item.description: Christian nationalist leaders are telling followers that Vice President Kamala Harris is under the influence of a “Jezebel spirit,” using a term with deeply racist and misogynistic roots that is setting off alarm bells for religious and political scholars.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT and MICHELLE R. SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP lawmaker in hurricane-ravaged Georgia demands emergency session of Congress
item.description: Rep. Buddy Carter is urging Congressional leaders to reconvene lawmakers in Washington, D.C., to deal with funding for disaster victims after the hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chutkan unseals much-redacted Jack Smith evidence used to build Trump's Jan. 6 case
item.description: A judge on Friday unsealed nearly 1,900 pages of evidence special counsel Jack Smith assembled in building the election interference case against former President Trump, publicly posting the highly redacted trove.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump campaign slams 'witch hunt' after Jack Smith court docs released less than 3 weeks before election
item.description: The judge in former President Trump's federal election interference case on Friday made public more documents from Special Counsel Jack Smith's investigation into the former president just weeks before the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Submit your questions for the JD Vance town hall on NewsNation
item.description: X users can use the hashtag #VanceTownHall to submit questions to JD Vance, moderated by Chris Cuomo.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Nevada Senate debate
item.description: Sen. Jacky Rosen (D-Nev.) and Republican Sam Brown duked it out during their first and only debate Thursday night, making their pitches to voters in one of the key battleground states on the 2024 map and a must-win state for Democratic Senate majority hopes.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump family will get 75% of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: How Republicans mainstreamed the baseless idea of noncitizen voting in 2024
item.description: Donald Trump and his allies have zeroed in on the baseless claim that Democrats are encouraging newly arrived migrants to vote for them in the 2024 election. There is no evidence of a plot like this.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block and Miles Parks, MPR News

item.title: Following death of Hamas leader, Harris says it's 'time for the day after to begin' in Gaza
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Kamala Harris said Thursday that Israel's killing of Hamas' top leader offers "an opportunity to finally end the war in Gaza," talking plainly about next steps even as the pro-Palestinian demonstrators who gathered outside her campaign event underscored the complicated politics at p
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, COLLEEN LONG and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Lydia Dominguez talks leadership, safety, and accountability in school board run for District B
item.description: Lydia Dominguez, candidate for the Clark County School Board in District B, joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to outline her vision for the district, focusing on financial accountability, student safety, and parental involvement.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Candidate Karl Catarata talks education, safety, and accountability in Clark County School Board race
item.description: Karl Catarata, running for the District A trustee seat on the Clark County School Board, joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide for an interview to discuss his platform and vision for the district.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court Friday, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access — such as allowing it to be mailed without an in-person doctor’s visit.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about absentee, mail-in, and early voting
item.description: Various forms of casting election ballots have changed since the COVID-19 pandemic affected in-person turnout and voting
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump visits barbershop in the Bronx
item.description: THE BRONX, N.Y. (PIX11) – Former President Donald Trump visited a barbershop in the Bronx on Thursday. Trump stopped by Javiel's Barbershop on Castle Hill Avenue.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Illinois alderwoman claims she was arrested after criticizing the mayor
item.description: Alderwoman Colby Chapman said she spent five hours in jail, in what she calls a politically motivated abuse of power.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Nevada US Senate Debate: Jacky Rosen and Sam Brown
item.description: As the seconds tick away until Election Day on Nov. 5, Democratic incumbent Sen. Jacky Rosen and Republican challenger Sam Brown are set to debate the issues Nevadans are most concerned about heading into 2025 and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk commits $70 million to boost Donald Trump
item.description: Elon Musk plunged more than $70 million into helping Donald Trump and other Republicans win in November's election, making him one of the biggest donors to GOP causes this campaign season, according to campaign finance disclosures released this week.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN SLODYSKO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Carter, at age 100, casts his 2024 ballot by mail
item.description: His son Chip Carter said before the family gathering that his father had this election very much in mind.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Republican Party sues Hennepin County over election judges list
item.description: A state law requires each county in Minnesota select election judges from lists provided by the Republican and Democratic parties. A lawsuit claims Hennepin County violated state statute by not selecting judges from a GOP list.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump uses interview on economics to promote tariffs and riff on his favorite themes
item.description: Donald Trump has seized on an opening to sound his frequent argument that imposing huge tariffs on foreign goods would amount to an economic elixir — one he claims would raise enormous sums for the government, protect U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Nevada DMV fast-tracks credentials for potential voters
item.description: The Nevada Department of Motor Vehicles is seeking to move potential voters to the front of the line starting next week and is reminding new residents they can surrender their out-of-state ID.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Johnson: Additional hurricane aid 'can wait' until Congress is back in session
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Sunday said passing additional hurricane aid for states impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton “can wait” until Congress is back in session after the election.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC
